title: Weak SSL/TLS protocols should not be used
description: The use of weak SSL/TLS protocols (such as SSLv2, SSLv3, TLS 1.0, or TLS 1.1) exposes data in transit to interception and decryption by attackers. Only strong, up-to-date protocols (such as TLS 1.2 or higher) should be used to ensure confidentiality and integrity of communications.
message: Weak SSL/TLS protocol detected. Use only strong, secure protocols (TLS 1.2+).
impact: Weak protocols can be exploited by attackers to decrypt sensitive data, perform man-in-the-middle attacks, or bypass security controls.
recommendation: Configure all services and resources to use only strong protocols (TLS 1.2 or higher). Disable support for deprecated protocols in your infrastructure and application settings.

noncompliant.description: Listener is configured to allow TLS 1.0, which is considered weak.

compliant.description: Listener is configured to allow only TLS 1.2, which is strong.

devsecops.security: Ensures data in transit is protected from eavesdropping and tampering.
devsecops.automation: Automated checks should enforce strong protocol usage in CI/CD pipelines.
devsecops.compliance: Meets regulatory requirements for secure data transmission (e.g., PCI DSS, HIPAA, GDPR).

why_is_this_an_issue: Weak SSL/TLS protocols are vulnerable to known attacks (e.g., POODLE, BEAST, downgrade attacks) and do not provide adequate protection for sensitive data.
how_to_fix: Update all configurations to use only strong protocols (TLS 1.2 or higher). Remove or disable support for SSLv2, SSLv3, TLS 1.0, and TLS 1.1.

security_mappings.CERT: MSC61-J: Do not use insecure or weak cryptographic algorithms
